summary: Lookalike @onerjs scope republishing BabylonJS smart-filters-blocks with attacker-namespaced dependencies
details: Package impersonates @babylonjs/smart-filters-blocks under the @onerjs scope while retaining the original Babylon README, repository URL (git+https://github.com/BabylonJS/Babylon.js.git), and replicating the public API surface. The package.json declares a hard dependency on @onerjs/smart-filters@8.52.4 and a peer dependency on @onerjs/core (^7.47.3 || ^8.0.1) — both impersonating real @babylonjs/* packages under the same lookalike scope. While this specific tarball contains no exfiltration or RCE code, installing it forces resolution of the @onerjs/smart-filters sibling, pulling attacker-controlled code from a non-Babylon-publisher namespace into the installer's dependency tree. The scope name (@onerjs vs @babylonjs) is a one-letter homoglyph-style edit of a well-known publisher, and the intact Babylon branding indicates intent to confuse installers searching for the legitimate library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
